title: Winston Churchill wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Sir Winston Leonard Spencer Churchill was a British statesman, military officer, and writer who was Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1940 to 1945, during the Second World War, and again from 1951 to 1955.

title: Bob Dylan wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Bob Dylan is an American singer-songwriter.

title: Ernest Hemingway wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Ernest Miller Hemingway was an American novelist, short-story writer and journalist.

title: Albert Camus wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Albert Camus was a French philosopher, novelist, author, dramatist, journalist, world federalist, and political activist.

title: John Steinbeck wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: John Ernst Steinbeck was an American writer and novelist.

title: Rabindranath Tagore wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Rabindranath Thakur, also known by his pseudonym Bhanusimha was a Bengali polymath of the Bengal Renaissance period.

title: Toni Morrison wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Chloe Anthony Wofford "Toni" Morrison was an American novelist and editor.

title: Rudyard Kipling wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Joseph Rudyard Kipling was an English journalist, novelist, poet and short-story writer.

title: Bertrand Russell wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Bertrand Arthur William Russell, 3rd Earl Russell, was an English philosopher, logician, mathematician, and public intellectual.

title: T. S. Eliot wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Thomas Stearns Eliot was a poet, essayist and playwright.

title: Jean-Paul Sartre wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Jean-Paul Charles Aymard Sartre was a French philosopher, playwright, novelist, screenwriter, political activist, biographer, and literary critic, considered a leading figure in 20th-century French philosophy and Marxism.

title: William Faulkner wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: William Cuthbert Faulkner was an American writer.

title: Gabriel García Márquez wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Gabriel José García Márquez was a Colombian writer and journalist, known affectionately as Gabo or Gabito throughout Latin America.

title: George Bernard Shaw wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: George Bernard Shaw, known at his insistence as Bernard Shaw, was an Irish playwright, critic, polemicist and political activist.

title: Samuel Beckett wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Samuel Barclay Beckett was an Irish playwright, poet, novelist, and literary critic.

title: William Butler Yeats wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: William Butler Yeats was an Irish poet, dramatist, writer and literary critic who was one of the foremost figures of 20th-century literature.

title: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Aleksandr Isayevich Solzhenitsyn was a Soviet and Russian author and dissident who helped to raise global awareness of political repression in the Soviet Union, especially the Gulag prison system.

title: Thomas Mann wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Paul Thomas Mann was a German novelist, short story writer, social critic, philanthropist, essayist, and the 1929 Nobel Prize in Literature laureate.

title: Hermann Hesse wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Hermann Karl Hesse was a German-Swiss poet and novelist, and winner of the 1946 Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: Pablo Neruda wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Pablo Neruda was a Chilean poet-diplomat and politician who won the 1971 Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: Mario Vargas Llosa wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Jorge Mario Pedro Vargas Llosa, 1st Marquess of Vargas Llosa was a Peruvian novelist, journalist, essayist and politician.

title: Orhan Pamuk wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Ferit Orhan Pamuk is a Turkish novelist, screenwriter, academic, and recipient of the 2006 Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: John Galsworthy wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: John Galsworthy was an English novelist and playwright.

title: Pearl S. Buck wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Pearl Comfort Sydenstricker Buck was an American writer and humanitarian.

title: Doris Lessing wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Doris May Lessing was a British novelist – sometimes identified as Rhodesian early in her career – and winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2007.

title: Boris Pasternak wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Boris Leonidovich Pasternak was a Russian and Soviet poet, novelist, and literary translator.

title: Alice Munro wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Alice Ann Munro was a Canadian short story writer who was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2013.

title: Henri Bergson wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Henri-Louis Bergson was a French philosopher who was influential in the traditions of analytic philosophy and continental philosophy, especially during the first half of the 20th century until the Second World War, but also after 1966 when Gilles Deleuze published Le Bergsonisme.

title: José Saramago wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: José de Sousa Saramago was a Portuguese writer.

title: André Gide wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: André Paul Guillaume Gide was a French author whose writing spanned a wide variety of styles and topics.

title: Gabriela Mistral wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Lucila Godoy Alcayaga, known by her pseudonym Gabriela Mistral, was a Chilean poet-diplomat, journalist and educator.

title: Günter Grass wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Günter Wilhelm Grass was a German novelist, poet, playwright, illustrator, graphic artist, sculptor, and recipient of the 1999 Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: Naguib Mahfouz wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Naguib Mahfouz Abdelaziz Ibrahim Ahmed Al-Basha was an Egyptian writer who won the 1988 Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: Yasunari Kawabata wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Yasunari/Kōsei Kawabata was a Japanese novelist and short story writer whose spare, lyrical, subtly-shaded prose works won him the 1968 Nobel Prize in Literature, the first Japanese author to receive the award.

title: Knut Hamsun wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Knut Hamsun was a Norwegian writer who was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1920.

title: Selma Lagerlöf wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Selma Ottilia Lovisa Lagerlöf was a Swedish writer.

title: Svetlana Alexievich wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Svetlana Alexandrovna Alexievich is a Belarusian investigative journalist, essayist and oral historian who writes in Russian.

title: Octavio Paz wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Octavio Paz Lozano was a Mexican philosopher, poet, and diplomat.

title: Joseph Brodsky wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Iosif Aleksandrovich Brodsky, anglicized as Joseph, was a Russian and American poet and essayist.

title: Nadine Gordimer wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Nadine Gordimer was a South African writer and political activist.

title: Luigi Pirandello wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Luigi Pirandello was an Italian dramatist, novelist, poet, and short story writer most noted for his plays.

title: Elfriede Jelinek wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Elfriede Jelinek is an Austrian playwright and novelist.

title: Anatole France wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Anatole France was a French poet, journalist, and novelist with several best-sellers.

title: Henryk Sienkiewicz wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Henryk Adam Aleksander Pius Sienkiewicz, also known by the pseudonym Litwos, was a Polish epic writer.

title: Romain Rolland wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Romain Rolland was a French dramatist, novelist, essayist, art historian and mystic who was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1915 "as a tribute to the lofty idealism of his literary production and to the sympathy and love of truth with which he has described different types of human beings".

title: Herta Müller wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Herta Müller is a Romanian-German novelist, poet, essayist and recipient of the 2009 Nobel Prize in Literature.

title: Theodor Mommsen wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Christian Matthias Theodor Mommsen was a German classical scholar, historian, jurist, journalist, politician and archaeologist.

title: Ivan Bunin wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Ivan Alekseyevich Bunin was the first Russian writer awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature, in 1933.

title: Maurice Maeterlinck wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Maurice Polydore Marie Bernard Maeterlinck, also known as Count/Comte Maeterlinck from 1932, was a Belgian playwright, poet, and essayist who was Flemish but wrote in French.

title: Grazia Deledda wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Grazia Maria Cosima Damiana Deledda was an Italian writer who received the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1926 "for her idealistically inspired writings which with plastic clarity picture the life on her native island [i.e.

title: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Bjørnstjerne Martinius Bjørnson was a Norwegian writer who received the 1903 Nobel Prize in Literature "as a tribute to his noble, magnificent and versatile poetry, which has always been distinguished by both the freshness of its inspiration and the rare purity of its spirit".

title: Gerhart Hauptmann wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Gerhart Johann Robert Hauptmann was a German dramatist and novelist.

title: Giosuè Carducci wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Giosuè Alessandro Giuseppe Carducci was an Italian poet, writer, literary critic and teacher.

title: Frédéric Mistral wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: Joseph Étienne Frédéric Mistral was an Occitan writer and lexicographer of the Provençal form of the language.

title: Sully Prudhomme wins the Nobel Prize in Literature
fact: René François Armand "Sully" Prudhomme was a French poet and essayist.
